free_text: Excitation functions of residues in the interaction
of 12C with 103Rh up to an incident energy of 400 MeV
12C beam at energies ranging between 93 MeV to 400 MeV
at iThemba LABS (former National Accelerator Centre)
the irradiations were performed at the Laboratori
Nazionale del Sud (LNS) in Catania, Italy.
The 103Rh targets were thin (310 mu-g/cm2) foils
deposited on a 1.5 mu-m thick mylar backing.
In the irradiations performed at the iThemba LABS
stacked foils of 103Rh interspaced with aluminium
catchers and degraders were used. In total four
* at 151.3 MeV a stack made of nine 103Rh foils
* at 225.8 MeV the stack was made of 16 rhodium
* at 320.2 MeV the stack was made of 16 rhodium foils
* at 399 MeV the stack was made of 16 rhodium foils
interspaced with aluminium catchers and degraders
of the same thickness as in the third irradiation.
The gamma activity induced in the target and
the catchers was measured for several weeks after the
irradiation by means of high efficiency HPGe detectors.
Considering all the possible sources of error,
the  uncertainty in the absolute values of the angle-
and range-integrated cross sections is estimated to be
The uncertainty in the thicknesses of the
the uncertainty in thickness of the catchers
the uncertainty of the HPGe efficiency (5%);
Data were received from Prof. E.Gadioli
